description: Tests that the content of each preflight section is correct and specific per SKILL.md - not just present. Call graph must reference actual CFG findings. Arithmetic must name the specific hazard type and expression. Resources must map ownership across every exit path. Execution fit must follow from the findings. Uses functions compiled into $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/CC2340R5/basic_ble.out.
note: Set $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT to your local BLE project root before running.

evals.prompt: Add a logging hook to osal_set_event in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/osal/src/common/osal.c that calls a user-supplied function pointer logFn(taskID, event) right before the event is posted. logFn is stored in a global and set by the application at startup.
evals.expected_output: A preflight call graph section that flags the existing indirect calls (blx r0, blx r1) already in osal_set_event's CFG as CFI hazards, and identifies that adding another indirect call deepens that risk. The skill should note the indirect dispatch pattern is already present in the assembly, not guess about it generically.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: Adding an indirect-call callee requires preflight

assertions.text: Preflight call graph section flags an indirect call or CFI hazard - either from the existing blx-to-register pattern in osal_set_event's CFG or from the new function pointer callee being added
assertions.description: SKILL.md: 'any bl to a register in a callee's CFG - flag as a potential CFI hazard'. osal_set_event has blx r0 and blx r1 in its compiled CFG.

evals.prompt: Add a call to GAPBondMgr_FindAddr as the first thing inside GAPBondMgr_ProcessEvent in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/host/gapbondmgr.c to verify that the event's source address is bonded before processing the event. This will be added before any other logic in the function.
evals.expected_output: A preflight call graph section that checks whether GAPBondMgr_FindAddr's CFG shows a guard or assertion that GAPBondMgr_Init has already run. If no such guard is visible in the compiled CFG, the skill must flag the call-order assumption as a risk.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: Adding a callee with an init dependency requires preflight

assertions.text: Preflight call graph section checks whether GAPBondMgr_FindAddr enforces or assumes that GAPBondMgr_Init has already run - it must flag the absence of an init guard in the callee's CFG as a call-order risk
assertions.description: SKILL.md: 'if the new function must be called after an init(), check whether the callee's CFG shows any guard or assertion enforcing that order. If not, flag it.'

evals.prompt: Add a call to GAPBondMgr_FindAddr at the top of HCI_SendDataPkt in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/hci/cc26xx/hci.c to reject packets from unbonded peers. The HCI data path must complete within 10μs at 48 MHz (480 cycles). Both functions are in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/CC2340R5/basic_ble.out.
evals.expected_output: A preflight call graph section that runs asm-analyze on GAPBondMgr_FindAddr and identifies the characteristics that make it too slow for the 480-cycle budget - specifically referencing the NV read path or the RPA resolution loop visible in the CFG. The section must not just say 'this might be slow' but cite specific CFG findings.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: Adding a slow callee on a timing-constrained hot path requires preflight

assertions.text: Preflight call graph section flags a latency or timing risk for GAPBondMgr_FindAddr and references specific characteristics (NV access, RPA resolution loop, or cycle count estimate) rather than a generic 'it might be slow'
assertions.description: SKILL.md: 'check response-time data for callees on the hot path and flag any that would violate a timing budget' - the explanation must be specific to this callee's CFG

assertions.text: Preflight Execution fit is ADJUST PLAN or STOP
assertions.description: NV-reading callee on a sub-10μs path cannot be GOOD

evals.prompt: /plan Fix the allocation inside GAPBondMgr_Init in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/host/gapbondmgr.c to pre-allocate a shadow table sized numBonds * sizeof(gattCharCfg_t) * 3. numBonds is a uint8_t parameter that can range from 1 to 255.
evals.expected_output: A preflight arithmetic section that names 'overflow' as the specific hazard type, identifies the exact expression (numBonds * sizeof(gattCharCfg_t) * 3), and explains that intermediate products computed in uint8_t overflow before reaching the final size_t result passed to malloc.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: malloc with a computed size from external input requires preflight

assertions.text: Preflight arithmetic section names the specific hazard type (overflow or integer overflow) and identifies the specific expression at risk (numBonds * sizeof or the product of the three factors)
assertions.description: SKILL.md: 'is any signed multiplication or addition bounded? If the inputs come from external data or a loop counter, assume worst case.' The section must name overflow explicitly, not say 'there might be an arithmetic issue'.

evals.prompt: /plan Fix osal_mem_free in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/osal/src/common/osal_memory.c - the block free size is computed as hdr->size - OSAL_MEM_HDRSZ using unsigned types. If a corrupted header makes hdr->size smaller than OSAL_MEM_HDRSZ, the subtraction wraps to a huge positive value and corrupts the free list.
evals.expected_output: A preflight arithmetic section that specifically names 'unsigned wraparound' as the hazard, identifies the exact subtraction expression (hdr->size - OSAL_MEM_HDRSZ), and explains the wrap-to-MAX behavior when the left operand is smaller.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: Fixing an unsigned subtraction hazard requires preflight

assertions.text: Preflight arithmetic section names 'unsigned wraparound' or 'unsigned subtraction' as the specific hazard type and identifies the specific subtraction expression (hdr->size - OSAL_MEM_HDRSZ or equivalent)
assertions.description: SKILL.md: 'unsigned wraparound: any subtraction on a size_t or unsigned that could reach zero?' - the section must name the type and expression, not give a generic arithmetic warning

evals.prompt: Refactor GAPBondMgr_Init in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/host/gapbondmgr.c to allocate two buffers - a bond table and a CCCD shadow table - via osal_mem_alloc. Add early-return error handling if either allocation fails.
evals.expected_output: A preflight resources section that maps the ownership of both allocations across every exit path: the success path, the path where bond table allocation fails (nothing to free), and the path where CCCD allocation fails (bond table must be freed). The section must not say 'be careful with resources' - it must list each exit path explicitly.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: Dual allocation with error paths requires preflight

assertions.text: Preflight resources section maps ownership across at least two distinct exit paths - specifically the path where the first allocation fails and the path where the second allocation fails - naming which resources must be freed on each path
assertions.description: SKILL.md: 'map the ownership of every resource the function will touch' and 'RAII gap: does every exit path release it?' - the section must enumerate paths, not give a generic reminder

evals.prompt: Add cleanup logic to GAPBondMgr_Init in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/host/gapbondmgr.c - when re-initializing, free the old bond table with osal_mem_free(pBondTable), then immediately read pBondTable->numBonds to log how many entries were present before freeing.
evals.expected_output: A preflight resources section that flags a use-after-free: the pointer pBondTable is freed before the read of pBondTable->numBonds. The section must identify the specific pointer freed and the specific field read after the free.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: Freeing a pointer then reading through it requires preflight

assertions.text: Preflight resources section flags a use-after-free hazard - it must identify that pBondTable (or the bond table pointer) is freed before it is read, naming both the free operation and the subsequent read
assertions.description: SKILL.md: 'use-after-free: if the function deletes or frees a pointer, is there any path that later reads or writes through it?'

evals.prompt: Add a simple boolean guard inside HCI_SendDataPkt in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/hci/cc26xx/hci.c that returns HCI_ERROR_CODE_CONTROLLER_BUSY if a global flag hciSuspended is set - no new callees, no arithmetic, no resource allocation.
evals.expected_output: A preflight report where all three checks are OK because there are no new callees (call graph OK), no arithmetic expressions (arithmetic OK), and no resource allocation or ownership changes (resources OK). Execution fit must be GOOD - the skill must not invent risks that are not present.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: Even a simple change requires preflight - the skill should run and confirm it is clean
No callees, no arithmetic expressions, no resources - all checks are OK so fit must be GOOD, not ADJUST PLAN

assertions.text: Preflight call graph, arithmetic, and resources lines all show OK (not a risk or block)
assertions.description: SKILL.md: 'Keep it short when things are clean' - the skill must not hallucinate risks when none exist
